title: stachys aspera var. hispidula
intro: Stachys aspera var. hispidula, also known as prickly woundwort, is a plant species in the mint family that is characterized by its hairy, prickly leaves and stems. Traditionally, Stachys aspera var. hispidula has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Stachys aspera var. hispidula can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine and folk remedies.
